title: CoreGrowthPrompting/README.en.md at main · CoreGrowthLabs/CoreGrowthPrompting
excerpt: 非IT技術者のためのバイブコーディング手法CGP（Core Growth Prompting）/ A vibe-coding method & primer for non-engineers building software through AI dialogue - CoreGrowthLabs/CoreGrowthPrompting
content_html:
CGP (Core Growth Prompting) Primer

A primer for "CGP (Core Growth Prompting)" — a method used by someone with zero coding ability to design and implement a patent-pending system through dialogue with an AI alone.

The full background story is written up (in Japanese, for now) here: 👉 How a 40-something office worker who can't code built a patent-worthy system

What this is

A primer meant to be pasted at the very start of a new chat, so you don't have to re-explain your whole design philosophy from scratch every time. Loading it first gives the LLM a consistent frame to work from, which makes the conversation more reproducible.

What problem this solves

CGP was built to address a few specific problems:

  • When someone who can't code designs a system purely through dialogue with an AI, adding features one at a time tends to erode consistency over time, turning into an unplanned patchwork.
  • Every new chat means re-explaining the whole design philosophy from scratch, and premises quietly drift across sessions, which eventually causes real problems.
  • AI tends to state things confidently even when it's wrong (hallucination), so a non-engineer needs a set of judgment criteria they can apply without having to consciously run a verification process every time.

CGP addresses these with a paired set of things: a procedure — start from one concrete dissatisfaction or question, protect the core as untouchable, and attach new capabilities from the outside — and a standing set of criteria for what counts as verified and what doesn't.

How is this different from the "orchestrator (core) + worker modules" pattern?

The structure of "one persona that talks to the user, other modules are just functions that return structured output" is a well-known pattern in AI system design — often called orchestrator+worker or supervisor+worker. CGP didn't invent that pattern. What's different about CGP is the part that comes before you get to that structure.

  • Most explanations of orchestrator+worker are implementation guides written for engineers who already have a settled design. They don't address how to find the minimal core in the first place (Step 1: Core Discovery), or how to judge whether a new feature is safe to attach (Step 3: Attachment). CGP's focus is on the procedure for a non-coder to arrive at that structure through dialogue alone, starting from nothing, without destabilizing the design along the way.
  • CGP fixes a specific priority order — hallucination-resistance > robustness > low latency — and mechanically rejects any proposal that violates it. That's a value judgment baked directly into the method, not just an architectural choice. General descriptions of the orchestrator+worker pattern don't specify this kind of priority ordering.
  • Limiting worker modules to "functions that return structured output" rather than autonomous agents isn't unusual as an implementation choice. What's different is that CGP explicitly forbids relaxing that constraint, treating it as "breaking the core," and requires an explicit check — "does this break the core?" — before any change touches it.
  • Most importantly, CGP treats "when is this done" not as a matter of meeting a spec, but as a judgment call a human has to make (Step 4: Organic Completion). Architecture-pattern write-ups generally don't answer that question at all.

In short, CGP isn't a new software architecture. It's the procedure and judgment criteria that let a non-engineer arrive at that architecture through dialogue alone, and keep growing it without destabilizing it.

Two variants of the primer

CGP comes in two versions with different underlying premises. Pick the one that matches your situation.

Personal-use CGP CGP for high-publicness cases
Intended for Projects built by one person or a small team Contexts where multiple stakeholders may hold genuinely conflicting values (government, local communities, decisions involving multiple parties, etc.)
Core principle Keep one dialogue persona; prioritize grounding in fact Never resolve conflicts away — structure and preserve them
File Primer/cgp-primer-parsonal.en.md Primer/cgp-primer-public.en.md

If you're unsure which one applies, ask yourself: "Can a single builder (me) make the final call on this project?" If yes, use the personal-use version. If judgments could genuinely differ across multiple stakeholders, use the public-interest version.

The public-interest version isn't a light reskin of the personal one — it's designed as a separate method. Don't mix them; use whichever one actually matches your case.

How to use it

  1. Open a new chat
  2. Paste the full contents of the matching primer (see table above)
  3. Tell the LLM: "Please proceed with the design following the principles in this primer."
  4. Start describing what you want to build

An important caveat

These primers emerged from the process of building one specific system — "Prime Source," a system for verifying news articles against primary sources. They are not a universal answer. What's optimal depends heavily on what you're actually building.

Start by using one of these as-is to build something. Then, once you're comfortable with it, evolve your own version tailored to your own projects. These primers are meant as a starting point, not a final answer.

Contents

  • Primer/cgp-primer-parsonal.ja.md — Personal-use primer (Japanese)
  • Primer/cgp-primer-parsonal.en.md — Personal-use primer (English)
  • Primer/cgp-primer-public.ja.md — Primer for high-publicness cases (Japanese)
  • Primer/cgp-primer-public.en.md — Primer for high-publicness cases (English)

Roadmap

This repo will likely grow to include documentation on Prime Source — the information-audit system, built using CGP, that verifies news articles against primary sources using structural text analysis.

License

MIT License. Feel free to modify, redistribute, and use commercially. See LICENSE for details.

What I hope this leads to

My hope is that this lowers the barrier enough for more people to get involved in civic tech — that domain experts who understand a public problem firsthand can build trustworthy software around it, without needing a software engineering background first.

Did you build something with this?

If CGP helps you build something, I'd love to see it. If you'd like, share your project and use #BuiltWithCGP or #CoreGrowthPrompting. There's absolutely no obligation—I just enjoy seeing what people create with these ideas.

blocks.html_en: What problem this solves

sentences.en: What problem this solves
sentences.zh: 这解决了什么问题

blocks.html_en: CGP was built to address a few specific problems:

sentences.en: CGP was built to address a few specific problems:
sentences.zh: CGP 的建立是为了解决一些具体问题：

blocks.html_en: • When someone who can't code designs a system purely through dialogue with an AI, adding features one at a time tends to erode consistency over time, turning into an unplanned patchwork.

sentences.en: When someone who can't code designs a system purely through dialogue with an AI, adding features one at a time tends to erode consistency over time, turning into an unplanned patchwork.
sentences.zh: 当不会编码的人纯粹通过与人工智能对话来设计系统时，一次添加一个功能往往会随着时间的推移而侵蚀一致性，变成一种计划外的拼凑。

blocks.html_en: • Every new chat means re-explaining the whole design philosophy from scratch, and premises quietly drift across sessions, which eventually causes real problems.

sentences.en: Every new chat means re-explaining the whole design philosophy from scratch, and premises quietly drift across sessions, which eventually causes real problems.
sentences.zh: 每一次新的聊天都意味着从头开始重新解释整个设计理念，并且前提会悄悄地在会话之间漂移，这最终会导致真正的问题。

blocks.html_en: • AI tends to state things confidently even when it's wrong (hallucination), so a non-engineer needs a set of judgment criteria they can apply without having to consciously run a verification process every time.

sentences.en: AI tends to state things confidently even when it's wrong (hallucination), so a non-engineer needs a set of judgment criteria they can apply without having to consciously run a verification process every time.
sentences.zh: 即使错误（幻觉），人工智能也倾向于自信地陈述事物，因此非工程师需要一套可以应用的判断标准，而不必每次都有意识地运行验证过程。

blocks.html_en: CGP addresses these with a paired set of things: a procedure — start from one concrete dissatisfaction or question, protect the core as untouchable, and attach new capabilities from the outside — and a standing set of criteria for what counts as verified and what doesn't.

sentences.en: CGP addresses these with a paired set of things: a procedure — start from one concrete dissatisfaction or question, protect the core as untouchable, and attach new capabilities from the outside — and a standing set of criteria for what counts as verified and what doesn't.
sentences.zh: CGP 通过一组事物来解决这些问题：一个程序——从一个具体的不满或问题开始，保护核心不可触及，并从外部附加新的功能——以及一套关于什么被视为已验证、什么不被验证的标准。

blocks.html_en: How is this different from the "orchestrator (core) + worker modules" pattern?

sentences.en: How is this different from the "orchestrator (core) + worker modules" pattern?
sentences.zh: 这与“协调器（核心）+工作模块”模式有何不同？

blocks.html_en: The structure of "one persona that talks to the user, other modules are just functions that return structured output" is a well-known pattern in AI system design — often called orchestrator+worker or supervisor+worker. CGP didn't invent that pattern. What's different about CGP is the part that comes before you get to that structure.

sentences.en: The structure of "one persona that talks to the user, other modules are just functions that return structured output" is a well-known pattern in AI system design — often called orchestrator+worker or supervisor+worker.
sentences.zh: “一个角色与用户对话，其他模块只是返回结构化输出的函数”的结构是人工智能系统设计中的一种众所周知的模式——通常称为协调器+工人或主管+工人。

sentences.en: CGP didn't invent that pattern.
sentences.zh: CGP 并没有发明这种模式。

sentences.en: What's different about CGP is the part that comes before you get to that structure.
sentences.zh: CGP 的不同之处在于到达该结构之前的部分。

blocks.html_en: • Most explanations of orchestrator+worker are implementation guides written for engineers who already have a settled design. They don't address how to find the minimal core in the first place (Step 1: Core Discovery), or how to judge whether a new feature is safe to attach (Step 3: Attachment). CGP's focus is on the procedure for a non-coder to arrive at that structure through dialogue alone, starting from nothing, without destabilizing the design along the way.

sentences.en: Most explanations of orchestrator+worker are implementation guides written for engineers who already have a settled design.
sentences.zh: 大多数对orchestrator+worker的解释都是为已经有固定设计的工程师编写的实施指南。

sentences.en: They don't address how to find the minimal core in the first place (Step 1: Core Discovery), or how to judge whether a new feature is safe to attach (Step 3: Attachment).
sentences.zh: 他们没有解决如何首先找到最小核心（步骤 1：核心发现），或如何判断新功能是否可以安全附加（步骤 3：附加）。

sentences.en: CGP's focus is on the procedure for a non-coder to arrive at that structure through dialogue alone, starting from nothing, without destabilizing the design along the way.
sentences.zh: CGP 的重点是非编码人员从零开始仅通过对话即可达到该结构的过程，而不会破坏整个设计的稳定性。

blocks.html_en: • CGP fixes a specific priority order — hallucination-resistance > robustness > low latency — and mechanically rejects any proposal that violates it. That's a value judgment baked directly into the method, not just an architectural choice. General descriptions of the orchestrator+worker pattern don't specify this kind of priority ordering.

sentences.en: CGP fixes a specific priority order — hallucination-resistance > robustness > low latency — and mechanically rejects any proposal that violates it.
sentences.zh: CGP 确定了一个特定的优先级顺序——抗幻觉 > 鲁棒性 > 低延迟——并机械地拒绝任何违反它的提案。

sentences.en: That's a value judgment baked directly into the method, not just an architectural choice.
sentences.zh: 这是直接融入方法的价值判断，而不仅仅是架构选择。

sentences.en: General descriptions of the orchestrator+worker pattern don't specify this kind of priority ordering.
sentences.zh: Orchestrator+worker 模式的一般描述没有指定这种优先级排序。

blocks.html_en: • Limiting worker modules to "functions that return structured output" rather than autonomous agents isn't unusual as an implementation choice. What's different is that CGP explicitly forbids relaxing that constraint, treating it as "breaking the core," and requires an explicit check — "does this break the core?" — before any change touches it.

sentences.en: Limiting worker modules to "functions that return structured output" rather than autonomous agents isn't unusual as an implementation choice.
sentences.zh: 将工作模块限制为“返回结构化输出的函数”而不是自治代理作为实现选择并不罕见。

sentences.en: What's different is that CGP explicitly forbids relaxing that constraint, treating it as "breaking the core," and requires an explicit check — "does this break the core?" — before any change touches it.
sentences.zh: 不同的是，CGP 明确禁止放松该约束，将其视为“破坏核心”，并需要进行明确的检查 - “这会破坏核心吗？” ——在任何改变发生之前。

blocks.html_en: • Most importantly, CGP treats "when is this done" not as a matter of meeting a spec, but as a judgment call a human has to make (Step 4: Organic Completion). Architecture-pattern write-ups generally don't answer that question at all.

sentences.en: Most importantly, CGP treats "when is this done" not as a matter of meeting a spec, but as a judgment call a human has to make (Step 4: Organic Completion).
sentences.zh: 最重要的是，CGP 并不将“何时完成”视为满足规范的问题，而是将其视为人类必须做出的判断（步骤 4：有机完成）。

sentences.en: Architecture-pattern write-ups generally don't answer that question at all.
sentences.zh: 架构模式的文章通常根本无法回答这个问题。

blocks.html_en: In short, CGP isn't a new software architecture. It's the procedure and judgment criteria that let a non-engineer arrive at that architecture through dialogue alone, and keep growing it without destabilizing it.

sentences.en: In short, CGP isn't a new software architecture.
sentences.zh: 简而言之，CGP 并不是一种新的软件架构。

sentences.en: It's the procedure and judgment criteria that let a non-engineer arrive at that architecture through dialogue alone, and keep growing it without destabilizing it.
sentences.zh: 正是程序和判断标准让非工程师仅通过对话就可以达到该架构，并在不破坏其稳定性的情况下不断发展它。

blocks.html_en: My hope is that this lowers the barrier enough for more people to get involved in civic tech — that domain experts who understand a public problem firsthand can build trustworthy software around it, without needing a software engineering background first.

content_html_zh:
CGP（核心生长促进）入门

“CGP（核心增长提示）”入门书——一种由零编码能力的人使用的方法，通过单独与人工智能对话来设计和实现正在申请专利的系统。

完整的背景故事写在这里（目前是日语）：👉 一个不会编码的 40 多岁办公室职员如何构建了一个具有专利价值的系统

这是什么

底漆意味着在新聊天开始时粘贴，因此您不必每次都从头开始重新解释整个设计理念。 首先加载它可以为法学硕士提供一致的工作框架，从而使对话更具可重复性。

这解决了什么问题

CGP 的建立是为了解决一些具体问题：

  • 当不会编码的人纯粹通过与人工智能对话来设计系统时，一次添加一个功能往往会随着时间的推移而侵蚀一致性，变成一种计划外的拼凑。
  • 每一次新的聊天都意味着从头开始重新解释整个设计理念，并且前提会悄悄地在会话之间漂移，这最终会导致真正的问题。
  • 即使错误（幻觉），人工智能也倾向于自信地陈述事物，因此非工程师需要一套可以应用的判断标准，而不必每次都有意识地运行验证过程。

CGP 通过一组事物来解决这些问题：一个程序——从一个具体的不满或问题开始，保护核心不可触及，并从外部附加新的功能——以及一套关于什么被视为已验证、什么不被验证的标准。

这与“协调器（核心）+工作模块”模式有何不同？

“一个角色与用户对话，其他模块只是返回结构化输出的函数”的结构是人工智能系统设计中的一种众所周知的模式——通常称为协调器+工人或主管+工人。 CGP 并没有发明这种模式。 CGP 的不同之处在于到达该结构之前的部分。

  • 大多数对orchestrator+worker的解释都是为已经有固定设计的工程师编写的实施指南。 他们没有解决如何首先找到最小核心（步骤 1：核心发现），或如何判断新功能是否可以安全附加（步骤 3：附加）。 CGP 的重点是非编码人员从零开始仅通过对话即可达到该结构的过程，而不会破坏整个设计的稳定性。
  • CGP 确定了一个特定的优先级顺序——抗幻觉 > 鲁棒性 > 低延迟——并机械地拒绝任何违反它的提案。 这是直接融入方法的价值判断，而不仅仅是架构选择。 Orchestrator+worker 模式的一般描述没有指定这种优先级排序。
  • 将工作模块限制为“返回结构化输出的函数”而不是自治代理作为实现选择并不罕见。 不同的是，CGP 明确禁止放松该约束，将其视为“破坏核心”，并需要进行明确的检查 - “这会破坏核心吗？” ——在任何改变发生之前。
  • 最重要的是，CGP 并不将“何时完成”视为满足规范的问题，而是将其视为人类必须做出的判断（步骤 4：有机完成）。 架构模式的文章通常根本无法回答这个问题。

简而言之，CGP 并不是一种新的软件架构。 正是程序和判断标准让非工程师仅通过对话就可以达到该架构，并在不破坏其稳定性的情况下不断发展它。

引物的两种变体

CGP 有两个版本，具有不同的底层前提。 选择符合您情况的一项。

个人使用CGP 高公开案例的 CGP
适用于 由一个人或一个小团队构建的项目 多个利益相关者可能持有真正相互冲突的价值观的环境（政府、当地社区、涉及多方的决策等）
核心原则 保留一个对话角色；优先考虑事实基础 永远不要解决冲突——构建并保留它们
File 底漆/cgp-primer-parsonal.en.md 底漆/cgp-primer-public.en.md

如果您不确定哪一个适用，请问问自己：“单个建筑商（我）可以对这个项目做出最终决定吗？” 如果是，请使用个人使用版本。 如果多个利益相关者的判断确实存在差异，请使用公共利益版本。

公共利益版本并不是个人版本的简单改版——它被设计为一种单独的方法。 不要混合它们；使用与您的情况实际匹配的任何一个。

如何使用

  1. 打开新聊天
  2. 粘贴匹配引物的完整内容（见上表）
  3. 告诉法学硕士：“请按照本入门书中的原则继续设计。”
  4. 开始描述您想要构建的内容

一个重要的警告

这些入门书是在构建一个特定系统“Prime Source”的过程中产生的，这是一个根据主要来源验证新闻文章的系统。 它们并不是一个通用的答案。 什么是最佳的很大程度上取决于您实际构建的内容。

首先按原样使用其中之一来构建一些东西。 然后，一旦您适应了它，就可以根据自己的项目开发自己的版本。 这些引物只是一个起点，而不是最终答案。

内容

  • Primer/cgp-primer-parsonal.ja.md — 个人使用底漆（日语）
  • Primer/cgp-primer-parsonal.en.md — 个人使用底漆（英文）
  • Primer/cgp-primer-public.ja.md — 高公开案例的入门（日语）
  • Primer/cgp-primer-public.en.md — 高公开案例入门（英文）

路线图

该存储库可能会包含有关 Prime Source 的文档，Prime Source 是使用 CGP 构建的信息审核系统，可使用结构文本分析根据主要来源验证新闻文章。

执照

我的许可证。 请随意修改、重新分发和商业使用。 有关详细信息，请参阅许可证。

我希望这能带来什么

我希望这能够降低足够的门槛，让更多的人参与公民技术——直接了解公共问题的领域专家可以围绕它构建值得信赖的软件，而无需先具备软件工程背景。

你用这个构建了一些东西吗？

如果 CGP 可以帮助您构建一些东西，我很乐意看到它。 如果您愿意，请分享您的项目并使用#BuiltWithCGP 或#CoreGrowthPrompting。 绝对没有义务——我只是喜欢看到人们用这些想法创造出什么。
